title: DC13371351 Financial Analyst (6-month extendable contract)
description:
The Company

We are seeking a Financial Analyst with commodities experience to support finance operations across payment processing, invoicing, and transaction controls. This is 6 month contract with a high chance to be converted to permanent employee. The role sits close to day-to-day trading and operational activities and plays a key role in ensuring accurate financial recording, strong controls, and compliance with internal accounting policies. This position is well suited for candidates with experience in commodities trading, supply chain finance, or trading operations environments, who are comfortable working in a fast-paced, high-volume transactional setting.

The Role

Financial Operations & Payments

• Manage payments to and from vendors and customers, ensuring accuracy, completeness, and timeliness

• Review and approve payment requests in line with delegated authority and internal controls

• Raise customer invoices where required and follow up on outstanding balances

• Ensure all transactions are properly recorded in the financial systems

Transaction Processing & Controls

• Perform activities covered under ITP (Invoice-to-Pay) and ITC (Invoice-to-Cash) processes

• Ensure all activities are executed within the framework of financial controls and accounting policies

• Support month-end and period-end closing activities related to payments, receivables, and accruals

• Investigate and resolve discrepancies related to payments, invoices, and postings

Commodities & Trade Support

• Work closely with trading, operations, and supply chain teams to support commodity-related transactions

• Understand trade lifecycles including pricing, settlement, and counterparty payments

• Support reconciliation of trade-related accounts and cash movements

Governance & Continuous Improvement

• Maintain strong documentation of processes and controls

• Support audits (internal and external) by providing required documentation and explanations

• Identify opportunities to improve efficiency, automation, and control effectiveness within finance processes

Your Profile:

• Bachelor’s degree in finance/accounting

• Minimum 5 years of experience, prior experience in commodities trading, energy, metals, agriculture, or related sectors is strongly preferred.

• Strong understanding of financial controls and accounting principles

• Experience working with SAP is a strong plus.

• Advanced Excel skills: exposure to automation or reporting tools is a plus.

• Hands-on exposure to payments, invoicing, receivables, and payables processes

Apply Today

Please send your resume, in WORD format only and quote reference number DC13371351, by clicking the apply button. Please note that only short-listed candidates will be contacted.

Robert Half International Pte Ltd. Co. Registration no.: 200612189E | EA Licence no.: 07C5595 | Dmitriy Chervinskyi EA Registration no.: R22108655

postedCompany.description:
ROBERT HALF INTERNATIONAL PTE. LTD.


Founded in 1948, Robert Half (NYSE symbol: RHI) pioneered specialised recruitment services and today - with more than 325 staffing locations worldwide - Robert Half is the world's leader in placing accounting, finance, banking and technology professionals on a temporary and permanent basis.

In Singapore, Robert Half operates four divisions:

  • Robert Half Finance and Accounting: Recruitment services for temporary and permanent finance and accounting personnel
  • Robert Half Financial Services: Recruitment services for high-calibre financial services, insurance and banking professionals
  • Robert Half Management Resources: Recruitment services for senior-level finance, accounting and business systems professionals on a project and interim basis
  • Robert Half Technology: Recruitment services for temporary and permanent IT professionals

Robert Half also is the parent company of Protiviti®, a global consulting and internal audit firm composed of experts in risk, advisory and transaction services.
